questions.question: What type of programming language is Java (in terms of how it is executed)?
options: Purely interpreted at runtime like Python
Both compiled and interpreted — source is compiled to bytecode, then the JVM interprets or JIT-compiles it
Directly compiled to machine code like C
Executed as a shell script by the operating system
questions.answer: Both compiled and interpreted — source is compiled to bytecode, then the JVM interprets or JIT-compiles it

questions.question: Who created the Java programming language?
options: James Gosling and his team at Sun Microsystems
questions.answer: James Gosling and his team at Sun Microsystems

questions.question: In what year was Java first publicly released?
What company originally developed Java, and what company owns it today?
options: IBM originally developed it; Google owns it today
Microsoft originally developed it; Apache Foundation owns it today
Sun Microsystems originally developed it; Oracle owns it today
Netscape originally developed it; Amazon owns it today
questions.answer: Sun Microsystems originally developed it; Oracle owns it today

questions.question: What does the phrase 'Write Once, Run Anywhere' mean in the context of Java?
options: Java code can only be written once and cannot be modified after compilation
Java source code is automatically translated into every programming language
Java code, once compiled to bytecode, can run on any platform with a compatible JVM
Java programs are stored in the cloud and run from any browser
questions.answer: Java code, once compiled to bytecode, can run on any platform with a compatible JVM

questions.question: What is the Java Virtual Machine (JVM)?
options: A physical processor designed specifically for Java
A text editor used to write Java code
A virtual machine that executes Java bytecode, providing platform-independent execution
A cloud service that hosts Java applications
questions.answer: A virtual machine that executes Java bytecode, providing platform-independent execution

questions.question: Which of the following is a common real-world use of Java?
options: Android mobile app development and enterprise backend systems
questions.answer: Android mobile app development and enterprise backend systems

questions.question: What is the file extension for Java source code files?
questions.answer: .java

questions.question: What is the file extension for compiled Java bytecode files?
questions.answer: .class

questions.question: What terminal command compiles a Java source file?
questions.answer: javac

questions.question: What terminal command runs a compiled Java program?
questions.answer: java

questions.question: Why must the public class name in a Java file match the file name?
options: It is just a convention; the compiler does not enforce it
The JVM uses the class name to locate and organize class files correctly
It makes the code run faster
It is required only on Windows, not on macOS or Linux
questions.answer: The JVM uses the class name to locate and organize class files correctly

questions.question: What does the 'pwd' command do in the terminal?
options: Creates a new password for the system
questions.answer: Prints the current working directory

questions.question: What does the 'ls' command do?
options: Lists files and folders in the current directory
Logs the user out of the system
questions.answer: Lists files and folders in the current directory

questions.question: What does 'cd ..' do in the terminal?
options: Moves up one directory level to the parent directory
questions.answer: Moves up one directory level to the parent directory

questions.question: What is the difference between 'rm' and 'rm -r'?
options: 'rm' removes a single file; 'rm -r' removes a directory and all its contents recursively
'rm -r' is a safer version of 'rm' that asks for confirmation
There is no difference; they are aliases
questions.answer: 'rm' removes a single file; 'rm -r' removes a directory and all its contents recursively

questions.question: What does the 'mkdir' command do?
options: Moves a directory to a new location
questions.answer: Creates a new directory

questions.question: What does the 'touch' command do?
options: Opens a file in the default text editor
Creates a new empty file or updates the timestamp of an existing file
Displays the first 10 lines of a file
questions.answer: Creates a new empty file or updates the timestamp of an existing file

questions.question: What is the correct method signature for a Java program's entry point?
questions.answer: public static void main(String[] args)

questions.question: What Java statement is used to print text to the console?
questions.answer: System.out.println("text");

questions.question: What was Java's original name during its early development?
questions.answer: Oak

questions.question: Is Java an object-oriented programming language?
options: No, Java is a purely functional language
Yes, Java is a class-based, object-oriented programming language
No, Java is a procedural language like C
questions.answer: Yes, Java is a class-based, object-oriented programming language

questions.question: What does the 'cat' command do in the terminal?
options: Compiles and tests a source file
Creates a new file with default content
Displays the contents of a file in the terminal
questions.answer: Displays the contents of a file in the terminal

questions.question: What is the difference between 'cp' and 'mv' in the terminal?
options: 'cp' copies a file leaving the original in place; 'mv' moves or renames a file, removing it from the original location
'cp' works only on directories; 'mv' works only on files
There is no difference; both copy files
questions.answer: 'cp' copies a file leaving the original in place; 'mv' moves or renames a file, removing it from the original location

questions.question: What is bytecode in Java?
options: The original source code written by the programmer
A type of encryption used to secure Java applications
Platform-independent intermediate code produced by javac, stored in .class files, and executed by the JVM
Binary machine code that runs directly on the CPU
questions.answer: Platform-independent intermediate code produced by javac, stored in .class files, and executed by the JVM
